rationale: The cited 2010 JMKR paper is highly topically aligned: it links customer satisfaction to firm-value outcomes including abnormal return and idiosyncratic risk, which are also central dependent variables in the 2013 citing paper. The citing note explicitly positions its contribution as extending prior marketing-finance work from mean consumer evaluations to dispersion effects, making this citation plausibly foundational rather than incidental. With this overlap and no evidence of a purely perfunctory mention, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
